phrases.text_local: À quelle heure met-on le Jet d'Eau en marche ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah kel UR meh-TOHN luh ZHEH DOH on MARSH
phrases.cultural_note: The Jet d'Eau shoots water 140 metres into the air. It operates daily from March to October and on special occasions in winter.

responses.english: From March to October, during the day.
responses.phonetic: duh MARS ah ok-TOBR, ahn zhoor-NAY
responses.local: De mars à octobre, en journée.

phrases.text_local: D'où peut-on voir le Jet d'Eau sous son meilleur angle ?
phrases.pronunciation: DOO puh-TOHN vwar luh ZHEH DOH soo sohn meh-YEUR AHN-gluh
phrases.cultural_note: The fountain is visible from the Pont du Mont-Blanc and from the Bains des Pâquis pier, each offering a different perspective.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if you can walk near the fountain
phrases.text_local: Est-il possible de se promener sur la jetée près du Jet d'Eau ?
phrases.pronunciation: eh-TEEL poh-SEE-bluh duh suh prohm-NAY sur lah zhuh-TAY preh doo ZHEH DOH
phrases.cultural_note: A narrow pier leads close to the fountain, but visitors should expect to get wet on windy days — it is considered part of the experience.

responses.english: Yes, but watch out for the spray.

phrases.intent_en: Say the spray is reaching you
phrases.text_local: Les embruns nous atteignent — c'est rafraîchissant !
phrases.pronunciation: lay ZOM-brun nooz ah-TEHN-yuh — seh rah-freh-shee-SOHN
phrases.cultural_note: On windy days the mist from the Jet d'Eau can drench bystanders on the Pont du Mont-Blanc, which Genevans find entirely normal.

responses.english: Yes, it gets wet when it's windy!
responses.phonetic: wee, sah MOO-yuh kahn eel ee ah doo VAHN
responses.local: Oui, ça mouille quand il y a du vent !

phrases.intent_en: Ask how high the fountain shoots
phrases.text_local: Le jet d'eau monte à quelle hauteur ?
phrases.pronunciation: luh ZHEH DOH mohnt ah kel oh-TUHR
phrases.cultural_note: The Jet d'Eau shoots water up to 140 metres, making it one of the tallest fountains in the world.
